openmeter/billing/creditgrant.purpose: Credit-grant-centric facade over the charges layer exposing Create, Get, List, and UpdateExternalSettlement for creditpurchase.Charge values. Owns input validation and customer-ownership checks but holds no Ent client — all persistence flows through the injected charges.Service.

patterns.name: Config+Validate/New constructor
patterns.description: Every concrete implementation uses a Config struct with a Validate() method; New() calls Validate() before returning the service.
patterns.example: func New(c Config) (Service, error) { if err := c.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; return &service{chargesService: c.ChargesService}, nil }

patterns.name: input.Validate() called at top of every method
patterns.description: Each public service method calls input.Validate() before any business logic and wraps errors in models.NewNillableGenericValidationError.
patterns.example: func (s *service) Get(ctx context.Context, input GetInput) (creditpurchase.Charge, error) { if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return creditpurchase.Charge{}, err }; ... }

patterns.name: Delegate all persistence to charges.Service
patterns.description: No Ent imports appear here; all DB access flows through charges.Service or creditpurchase.Service injected via Config.
patterns.example: charge, err := s.chargesService.GetByID(ctx, charges.GetByIDInput{ID: input.ChargeID})

patterns.name: Customer ownership check in service layer
patterns.description: Get-style methods assert cpCharge.Intent.CustomerID == input.CustomerID after fetching; not enforced by charges.Service.
patterns.example: if charge.Intent.CustomerID != input.CustomerID { return creditpurchase.Charge{}, models.NewGenericForbiddenError(fmt.Errorf("charge does not belong to customer")) }

patterns.name: NoopService compile-time guard
patterns.description: noop.go provides NoopService{} with var _ Service = NoopService{} ensuring interface compliance without business logic.
patterns.example: var _ Service = NoopService{}

key_file_guides.role: Defines the Service interface, all Input types with Validate(), FundingMethod enum, PurchaseTerms, GrantFilters, and the concrete service struct with New constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Input.Validate() must call models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(errs...)) — never return raw errors from validation. FundingMethod.Validate() must be called inside CreateInput.Validate().

key_file_guides.role: No-op implementation used when credit grants are disabled; returns zero-values with nil errors for all methods.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must stay in sync with the Service interface; update when new methods are added.

anti_patterns: Importing openmeter/ent/db or calling Ent builders directly — all DB access must flow through charges.Service
Skipping input.Validate() at the top of a new method
Returning a charge without verifying CustomerID ownership in Get-style methods
Using context.Background() instead of the caller-supplied ctx
Adding business logic inside toIntent()/toSettlement() conversion helpers beyond pure field mapping

decisions.decision: Service delegates entirely to charges.Service rather than holding its own adapter
decisions.rationale: Keeps a single authoritative write path for credit-purchase charges; avoids parallel DB access patterns that could bypass transaction discipline.

decisions.decision: Ownership check done in the service layer, not in charges.Service
decisions.rationale: charges.Service is a generic multi-customer service; customer scoping is a caller responsibility enforced here.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new service method with input validation, ownership check, and delegation

openmeter/billing/models.purpose: Organisational container for shared billing domain model sub-packages: creditsapplied (credit slice with currency-aware summation), externalid (invoice/line external-ID types and Ent mixin helpers), stddetailedline (detailed line base struct with Ent mixin and goderive equality), and totals (financial breakdown struct with Ent mixin and currency-precision rounding). No source files live directly in this folder.

patterns.name: Ent mixin co-located with domain model
patterns.description: Each sub-package defines both the Go domain struct (model.go) and the corresponding Ent mixin (mixin.go) in the same package to keep schema and type in sync.
patterns.example:
// model.go: type Totals struct { ... }
// mixin.go: func (Mixin) Fields() []ent.Field { ... }

patterns.name: Typed Setter/Getter generic interfaces over raw Ent mutations
patterns.description: externalid and totals expose generic Creator[T]/Setter[T]/Getter interfaces so adapters never call individual Set* Ent methods directly.
patterns.example: type Setter[T any] interface { SetTotals(T) *T }; func Set[T any](s Setter[T], t Totals) { ... }

patterns.name: Currency-precision rounding before persistence
patterns.description: Totals.RoundToPrecision(currency) must be called before any DB write to prevent numeric precision drift.
patterns.example: t = t.RoundToPrecision(currencyx.Calculator{Currency: currency})

patterns.name: models.Validator on enums and structs
patterns.description: All enum types and structs in this package implement Validate() error conforming to models.Validator.
patterns.example: func (s Status) Validate() error { switch s { case ...: return nil; default: return fmt.Errorf("invalid status: %s", s) } }

patterns.name: goderive for equality on stddetailedline.Base
patterns.description: derived.gen.go is generated by goderive from annotations in generate.go; never hand-edit the generated file.
patterns.example: //go:generate go run github.com/awalterschulze/goderive . in generate.go

key_file_guides.role: Totals struct with Add, Sum, RoundToPrecision, Validate. Validate enforces non-negative invariants.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a field to Totals requires updating Add, RoundToPrecision, Validate, mixin Fields(), Setter[T], TotalsGetter, Set[T], and FromDB.

key_file_guides.role: Ent mixin adding invoicing/payment external ID columns; generic Creator/Updater/Getter interfaces for type-safe Ent mutations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always use lo.EmptyableToPtr for nullable string columns — never store empty strings.

key_file_guides.role: Base struct for flat-fee and usage-based detailed lines. child_unique_reference_id is required (DB CHECK constraint).
key_file_guides.watch_for: child_unique_reference_id has a DB CHECK constraint — it is required for upsert identity; never make it optional.

key_file_guides.role: CreditsApplied slice with Validate (positivity) and currency-aware Sum.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Accumulating amounts without RoundToPrecision causes precision drift.

key_file_guides.role: Goderive-generated equality for stddetailedline.Base — never hand-edit.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Regenerate via go generate after any Base struct field change.

anti_patterns: Adding DB/Ent logic to model.go or domain logic to mixin.go — keep layers strictly separated
Hand-editing derived.gen.go — always regenerate via go generate
Skipping RoundToPrecision before persisting Totals
Storing empty strings in nullable external-ID columns instead of using lo.EmptyableToPtr
Adding a new Totals field without updating all companion methods (Add, RoundToPrecision, Validate, mixin, Set, FromDB)

decisions.decision: Mixin and domain model co-located in the same sub-package rather than in a shared mixin layer
decisions.rationale: Keeps schema and domain type in sync; a single file change updates both the DB column and its Go representation.

decisions.decision: goderive for equality rather than hand-written Equal methods on stddetailedline.Base
decisions.rationale: Base has deeply nested pointer/slice fields; generated equality is complete and removes maintenance burden.

openmeter/billing/service.purpose: Concrete implementation of billing.Service — the central orchestrator for profiles, customer overrides, gathering and standard invoices, invoice lines, sequences, app resolution, lock acquisition, and the stateless-based invoice state machine. All DB access is delegated to billing.Adapter via entutils transactions.

patterns.name: transactionForInvoiceManipulation wraps all customer-mutating operations
patterns.description: All methods that create/update invoices or lines call transactionForInvoiceManipulation which first UpsertCustomerLock (outside tx) then starts a transaction and calls LockCustomerForUpdate inside it.
patterns.example: return transactionForInvoiceManipulation(ctx, s, input.Customer, func(ctx context.Context) (T, error) { ... })

patterns.name: transaction.Run / transaction.RunWithNoValue for all adapter calls
patterns.description: Service methods wrap adapter access in transaction.Run or RunWithNoValue so Ent's ctx-bound transaction is propagated correctly.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx context.Context) (billing.X, error) { return s.adapter.GetX(ctx, ...) })

patterns.name: InvoiceStateMachine + sync.Pool for state machine reuse
patterns.description: stdinvoicestate.go allocates InvoiceStateMachine instances from invoiceStateMachineCache (sync.Pool); external storage binds the state machine to the in-memory Invoice struct.
patterns.example: sm := invoiceStateMachineCache.Get().(*InvoiceStateMachine); defer invoiceStateMachineCache.Put(sm)

patterns.name: advancementStrategy switches between inline and queued advancement
patterns.description: advanceUntilStateStable checks s.advancementStrategy: QueuedAdvancementStrategy publishes an AdvanceStandardInvoiceEvent; otherwise runs sm.AdvanceUntilStateStable inline.
patterns.example: if s.advancementStrategy == billing.QueuedAdvancementStrategy { return s.publisher.Publish(ctx, billing.AdvanceStandardInvoiceEvent{...}) }

patterns.name: Config struct validation + New() constructor with RegisterLineEngine
patterns.description: service.go uses Config with Validate(); New() creates the service, instantiates billinglineengine.Engine internally, and calls RegisterLineEngine so the invoice engine is always pre-registered.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (*Service, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; svc := &Service{...}; invoiceEngine, _ := billinglineengine.New(...); svc.RegisterLineEngine(invoiceEngine); return svc, nil }

patterns.name: resolveTaxCodes with ReadOnly flag
patterns.description: taxcode.go's resolveTaxCodes uses ReadOnly=true for simulate/preview flows and ReadOnly=false for real invoice state machine transitions.
patterns.example: taxCodes, err := s.resolveTaxCodes(ctx, resolveTaxCodesInput{Namespace: ns, Invoice: out, ReadOnly: true})

patterns.name: featureMetersErrorWrapper converts not-found to ErrSnapshotInvalidDatabaseState
patterns.description: resolveFeatureMeters wraps the returned FeatureMeters so downstream callers receive ErrSnapshotInvalidDatabaseState instead of generic not-found for billing consistency.
patterns.example: return featureMetersErrorWrapper{featureMeters}, nil

key_file_guides.role: Service struct definition, Config validation, New constructor, transactionForInvoiceManipulation helper, WithAdvancementStrategy/WithLockedNamespaces options, RegisterStandardInvoiceHooks.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All invoice-mutating helpers must call transactionForInvoiceManipulation; never start a raw transaction without the customer lock sequence.

key_file_guides.role: InvoiceStateMachine with full state graph: draft → issuing → issued → payment states → paid/overdue/uncollectible/voided, plus delete and updating substates.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New states must be added to both allocateStateMachine() (Configure calls) and the StatusDetails resolver; the sync.Pool means the state machine is reused — always reset Invoice before use.

key_file_guides.role: InvoicePendingLines and prepareBillableLines — the core invoice-creation flow including progressive billing line splitting, collection cutoff resolution, and per-currency invoice creation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: resolvePendingLineCollectionCutoff has three alignment modes (bypass, subscription, anchored) — new alignment kinds must be added here.
Avg/Min meter aggregations are not supported for split lines — validate in getFeatureUsage.Validate(); semaphore size is maxParallelQuantitySnapshots from Config.

key_file_guides.role: Pure stateless calculation pipeline (sub-package); injected as invoicecalc.Calculator into Service; mockable via WithInvoiceCalculator.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All dependency data (FeatureMeters, TaxCodes, RatingService) must be resolved before calling Calculator.Calculate — the pipeline is stateless and cannot perform I/O.
Changing the default profile triggers customer re-pinning logic in handleDefaultProfileChange; all apps in a profile must have the same AppID (enforced by lo.Uniq check).

key_file_guides.role: resolveTaxCodes: collects unique tax codes from invoice lines and DefaultTaxConfig, looks them up or creates them via taxCodeService.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ReadOnly=true silently skips missing codes (for simulate); ReadOnly=false creates codes (for real flows) — never use ReadOnly=false from simulate paths.

key_file_guides.role: CreatePendingInvoiceLines: validates lines, resolves/upserts gathering invoice, normalizes and upserts lines, publishes GatheringInvoiceCreated event.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Lines with ChargeID set must have Engine explicitly set — validation enforces this; engine routing is done via lineEngines.populateGatheringLineEngine.

anti_patterns: Calling billing.Adapter methods directly without transaction.Run/RunWithNoValue — bypasses Ent ctx-bound transaction
Mutating customer invoices without calling transactionForInvoiceManipulation — omits advisory lock and leaves race conditions
Calling resolveTaxCodes with ReadOnly=false from simulate or preview code paths — creates tax code rows unexpectedly
Adding a new StandardInvoiceStatus without configuring it in allocateStateMachine() — state machine panics on unknown state
Importing billinglineengine from outside billingservice for direct line computation — always go through billing.Service.RegisterLineEngine and the LineEngine interface

decisions.decision: sync.Pool for InvoiceStateMachine to reduce GC pressure on high-frequency invoice advancement
decisions.rationale: Invoice state machines are large structs with closure-bound state graphs; pooling avoids repeated allocation on the hot billing-worker path.

decisions.decision: transactionForInvoiceManipulation UpsertCustomerLock outside the transaction then LockCustomerForUpdate inside
decisions.rationale: The advisory lock row must exist before the SELECT FOR UPDATE can acquire it; the two-step pattern prevents deadlocks when concurrent invoice creation races.

decisions.decision: advancementStrategy allows queued vs inline state machine advancement
decisions.rationale: Production uses queued advancement via billing-worker Kafka events to avoid holding long transactions; tests use inline advancement for determinism.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new invoice mutation method that must use the customer lock

openmeter/billing/validators.purpose: Organisational package grouping cross-domain billing guard validators that register pre/post-mutation hooks into customer.Service and subscription.Service, enforcing billing pre-conditions without introducing circular imports between the billing, customer, and subscription packages.

patterns.name: Registration at wiring time, not compile time
patterns.description: Each validator is constructed in app/common and registered via customerService.RegisterRequestValidator() or subscriptionService.RegisterHook(). Neither child imports the other domain's package at the call site — the coupling is in the DI layer only.
patterns.example: // app/common: NewValidator(...) then customerService.RegisterRequestValidator(v) or subscriptionService.RegisterHook(v)

patterns.name: Embed noop base structs
patterns.description: Validators embed customer.NoopRequestValidator or subscription.NoOpSubscriptionCommandHook so they only override the specific lifecycle methods they need.
patterns.example: type Validator struct { customer.NoopRequestValidator; billingService billing.Service; ... }

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion
patterns.description: Each validator file uses var _ Interface = (*Validator)(nil) to assert compliance at compile time.
patterns.example: var _ customer.RequestValidator = (*Validator)(nil)

patterns.name: Nil-guard constructors returning error
patterns.description: Both NewValidator functions check each injected service for nil and return an error, catching misconfigured wiring at startup.
patterns.example: if billingService == nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("billing service is required") }

patterns.name: models.NewGenericConflictError for billing setup failures
patterns.description: subscription/validator.go wraps validation errors in models.NewGenericConflictError so the HTTP layer renders a 409.
patterns.example: return models.NewGenericConflictError(fmt.Errorf("invalid billing setup: %w", err))

patterns.name: Sync-before-validate in customer deletion
patterns.description: customer/customer.go calls syncService.SynchronizeSubscription before listing invoices so pending subscription charges are reflected in the invoice state before the gate check runs.
patterns.example: v.syncService.SynchronizeSubscription(ctx, view, time.Now()) // must precede ListInvoices

patterns.name: errors.Join for multi-entity validation errors
patterns.description: customer/customer.go collects per-invoice errors into a slice and returns errors.Join(errs...) so the caller sees all blocking invoices at once.
patterns.example: errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("invoice %s not in final state", id)); return errors.Join(errs...)

key_file_guides.role: Implements customer.RequestValidator. ValidateDeleteCustomer syncs pending subscriptions then blocks deletion when non-final standard invoices or active gathering invoices exist.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Skipping the SynchronizeSubscription call before ListInvoices will cause false-negative gate checks; subscriptionService field is intentionally nullable — do not add nil guard without checking app/common wiring.

key_file_guides.role: Implements subscription.SubscriptionCommandHook via AfterCreate/AfterUpdate. Validates that the customer's installed billing app supports tax calculation, invoicing, and payment collection before a subscription with priced ratecards becomes active.
key_file_guides.watch_for: hasBillableItems short-circuits validation for free subscriptions — ensure this exit is still correct for new ratecard types; capability slice is hardcoded to three app.CapabilityType values.

anti_patterns: Adding ValidateCreateCustomer or ValidateUpdateCustomer in the customer validator without a billing-domain reason — general lifecycle belongs in the customer package
Calling billing.Adapter or Ent directly from either validator — all reads must go through billing.Service
Returning a plain error instead of models.NewGenericConflictError for billing setup failures in the subscription validator
Adding app-type-specific branching (e.g., 'if Stripe do X') in the subscription validator — capability validation is intentionally app-agnostic
Using context.Background() instead of propagating the incoming ctx parameter

decisions.decision: Validators live under billing/validators/ rather than inside customer/ or subscription/
decisions.rationale: Billing has dependency edges to both customer and subscription; placing validators here avoids import cycles by keeping billing-domain logic in billing's own package tree.

decisions.decision: Subscription sync driven inside the customer deletion validator rather than as a pre-hook in the subscription service
decisions.rationale: The sync ensures invoice state is up-to-date at the moment of deletion validation. Doing it inside the billing validator keeps the customer package free of billing concerns.

decisions.decision: Post-create/post-update (AfterCreate/AfterUpdate) hooks for subscription validation rather than pre-create
decisions.rationale: Billing setup validation checks state that only exists after the subscription is persisted (e.g. customer override profile, app assignment); a pre-create check would race against incomplete state.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new billing validator (embed noop, nil-guard constructor, compile-time assertion, wrap errors in GenericConflictError)
